site.name: Fort Myers Beach Insider
site.tagline: Own the beach. Use it when you want. Rent it when you don't.
site.positioning: Fort Myers Beach is a rebuilt, supply-constrained Gulf island in a 2026 buyer's market — the rare setup where lifestyle ownership and investment yield point at the same asset.
audience: Beach-property buyers in SW Florida and feeder markets (Midwest, Northeast, East Coast FL)
site.disclaimer: Informational only — not financial, legal, or tax advice. Data as of July 2026 from public sources; verify before transacting.

core_thesis.one_liner: Buy the 2026 price trough on an island with fixed land supply, a 41% hotel-room deficit, a completed $23M beach, and $1.7B of airport capacity being built 25 minutes away.
core_thesis.dual_use_pitch: A beach house here isn't either/or. Block the weeks you want. Rent the rest — weekly rentals are legal by right in the Gulf-side zone, and large new homes gross $80K–$166K a year.

pillars.title: Buyer's market entry
pillars.stat: Prices down 9–18% YoY; 95% of sales close below list; 11–13 months of supply

pillars.title: Structural lodging deficit
pillars.stat: Only ~59% of pre-Ian hotel rooms are back; county ADR $168, +10.2% YoY

pillars.title: Hardened new inventory
pillars.stat: Post-Ian elevated concrete builds carry materially lower insurance than legacy stock

pillars.title: Demand pipeline
pillars.stat: RSW: record 11.15M passengers 2025; 14 new gates by end of 2027

housing.condo_risks: Milestone inspections, SIRS reserve mandates, special assessments $20K–$100K+
housing.insurance_note: Legacy VE-zone flood $5K–$20K+/yr; new elevated builds materially cheaper; FEMA CRS Class 5 = 25% flood discounts island-wide; Citizens filed first rate decrease June 2026

rentals.rules: Weekly rentals allowed in Gulf-side exception zone; monthly minimum in residential zones; ~$300/yr registration; enforcement tightening
rentals.taxes: ~11%+ combined (state + county sales + 5% tourist development tax)

hotels.approved_pipeline: ~584 rooms/units (Arches Bayfront 263, Silver Sands 60, Pink Shell +40, others) — years from delivery

island_rebuild.beach: Renourishment complete Aug 2025 — $23M, ~1M cubic yards; 100K+ dune plantings
infrastructure: San Carlos Blvd $13M corridor complete — smart signals, seasonal transit lane
New storm-hardened Town Hall (Sept 2025); library reopened Mar 2025
Mooring field reopened (19 moorings, vessels to 85 ft)

development_pipeline.detail: $200M, 254 rooms, opened Dec 2023; refinanced 2026 on rising NOI
$17.7M, 1,000 ft (70% longer); contract awarded Apr 2026; completion 2027–28
$13M mixed-use; broke ground Jul 2026; opens mid-2027

island_rebuild.dining: 40+ restaurants open — Doc Ford's, Dixie Fish Co., Nervous Nellie's, Snug Harbor, Yucatan Beach Stand, Salty Sam's, The Whale (new elevated flagship, Nov 2025), six Margaritaville venues

rsw_airport.program: $1.7B terminal expansion — largest in Lee County Port Authority history; funded by $725M bond sale, no local tax dollars
rsw_airport.concourse_e: 14 new gates, ~$1.1B, halfway Dec 2025, target completion end of 2027
rsw_airport.status_rank: Top-50 U.S. airport; 2nd-busiest single-runway airport in America
rsw_airport.caveat: Phase 1 checkpoint completes 2029 — cite Concourse E (2027) as the near-term capacity win

lee_county.population: 875,607 (2025), +15.1% since 2020 — among America's fastest-growing large counties
lee_county.babcock_ranch: Record 1,066 home sales 2025 (+34%); top-5 U.S. master-planned community; never lost power in Ian
lee_county.lee_health: $820M, 236-bed Fort Myers hospital opening 2028 — SWFL's largest-ever healthcare build
lee_county.roads_2026: Cape Coral Bridge widening + SR 31 six-laning start construction; Big Carlos Pass Bridge open
lee_county.anchors: FGCU 16,600+ students; Red Sox + Twins spring training; Cape Coral's $125M Bimini Square opened Feb 2026

downtown_fort_myers.master_plan: $6B, 60+ acres of downtown/Midtown redevelopment over five years
downtown_fort_myers.news_press_site: 350 residences, 150K sq ft office, retail, 140–160-room hotel — construction starts Dec 2026
downtown_fort_myers.yacht_basin: Suntex Marinas — up to 280 slips, riverwalk, 40K sq ft dining/retail
downtown_fort_myers.delivered: Luminary Hotel ($91M, 243 rooms) + Caloosa Sound Convention Center
downtown_fort_myers.pipeline: 1,700+ residential units (Silver Hills/Triton Cay, Prima Luce, West End at City Walk, Vantage Lofts, more)
downtown_fort_myers.scene: Monthly Art Walk & Music Walk; Edison & Ford Winter Estates; 20 minutes from the beach

investment_ladder.note: SIRS/assessment/insurance risk — underwrite the building, not just the unit
Non-QM financing, 25%+ down; hands-off hotel-run rental
Heavier insurance load ($14K wind + $5–20K flood typical)

bottom_line.best_play: New-construction large homes on the walkable north end near Margaritaville/Times Square, in the weekly-rental zone
bottom_line.timing: Buy the 2026 trough — Realtor.com forecasts the metro -10.2% for 2026, the steepest in the U.S.; supply-constrained island fundamentals argue the other way long-term

sources: Lee County / Town of Fort Myers Beach
